source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Please choose the SMILES expression of the transition-state structure shown in the image, ignoring the arrows. <image_1>
extraction_notes: 答案字段为选项字母，已映射为对应选项文本
记录中只有 <image_1> 占位符和 PIL.Image 描述，未提供可提取的图片路径或 URL

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the transition-state structure is shown in the image
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual asset is central
the task is to choose a SMILES expression for a depicted structure, which cannot be recovered from text alone
multi_step: the solver must interpret the depicted chemical transition-state structure and then map it to one of 4 SMILES choices
the instruction to ignore arrows suggests selective visual parsing rather than direct transcription
this is more than a one-step text lookup, but the final answer is still a single multiple-choice match so complexity is moderate
verifiability: choices are provided and answer_text matches one option exactly
answer_type=short_text with a discrete chemistry string makes downstream checking straightforward

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the sample retains recoverable core semantics
the item is highly image-dependent, and the registry/scoring evidence says the depicted transition-state structure is central to solving it
the only explicit risk provided is small_image_resolution, which can reduce reliability for a visually central chemistry item
verifiability is high because the answer is a discrete short-text chemistry choice, so the sample is still worth retaining despite the image risk
